nodes.label: P3.0 — the objective swap (make-or-break gate)
nodes.description: Three unsupervised objectives on identical CIFAR-flat bench: energy-wall (P2.1 healthy cell) vs masked-reconstruction (hero, decoder D_l + mask + local MSE) vs contrast (CLAPP/InfoNCE). Pass gate: depth-slope >= 0 AND selectivity > 0.

nodes.label: P3.0's three-objective split
nodes.description: energy-wall: 0.332->0.198 (slope -0.020, decays through random floor). masked-recon: 0.174->0.136 (slope -0.005, ~flat, selectivity -0.062, BELOW random -- preserves density). contrast: 0.331->0.227 (slope -0.016, selectivity +0.060 growing +0.035->+0.073, ABOVE random -- preserves class but still declines).

nodes.label: P3.0 verdict: PARTIAL — objective family is the lever; contrast wins selectivity, needs coordination for slope
nodes.description: The objective family decides everything (reframe confirmed). Reconstruction is flat-but-wrong (preserves density); contrast is right-but-declining (preserves class, but each layer discriminates myopically with no cross-layer signal). Mono-Forward fallback NOT triggered.

nodes.label: Decision: carry contrastive objective -> route to P3.1 (coordination)
nodes.description: Masked-reconstruction rejected as proof that preservation-alone preserves the wrong thing. The residual contrast decline is a coordination gap (myopic per-layer discrimination), not an objective gap -- route to P3.1 (OLU / Direction 1) layered on the contrastive objective.
